rationale: The citing paper focuses on acute tryptophan depletion/serotonin effects on choice deferral and the compromise effect, while the cited paper focuses on ownership-moderated motivated perceptual judgment (wish vs. worry), so a direct substantive link is not clear from the notes. The matched evidence is only one DOI-level reference entry with no in-text citation context. Because evidence is sparse, this cannot be safely dropped as obvious mention-only, but it is not clearly a must-keep either.
